g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about the Characteristics of Database Approach, a fundamental concept in modern data management systems.
Traditional file processing stores data in separate files per application, causing redundancy and inconsistency issues.
g.text: Database Approach vs Traditional File Processing
Each app defines its own data

g.data-narration: The database approach offers nine key characteristics that distinguish it from traditional file systems.
The database is self-describing because it contains metadata about its own structure, stored in the data dictionary.
Data abstraction provides three levels: physical storage details, logical structure, and user-specific views.
Data independence allows changing schema at one level without affecting the next higher level, reducing maintenance costs.
Multiple views provide customized access to different users while maintaining security through virtual tables.
Database enables data sharing among multiple users with concurrency control mechanisms to handle simultaneous access.
Transaction processing ensures ACID properties: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability for reliable operations.
Data integrity is enforced through domain, entity, and referential constraints automatically by the DBMS.
Database security includes authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditing to protect sensitive information.
Centralized control gives DBAs authority to manage data as an organizational resource, eliminating redundancy.
g.text: Schema Management • Performance Monitoring • Backup & Recovery • Standards Enforcement

g.data-narration: This example shows how a university database provides different views for students, faculty, and administrators.
g.text: Example: Data Abstraction in University Database

g.data-narration: This banking example demonstrates how atomicity ensures either both debit and credit happen, or neither does.
g.text: BEGIN TRANSACTION → Debit Savings ₹10,000 → Credit Checking ₹10,000 → COMMIT
If system fails mid-transfer: ROLLBACK - No changes applied

g.data-narration: In summary, the database approach provides self-describing data, abstraction, independence, security, and reliable transaction processing.
